summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a wide range of topics including the purchase of a digital township sign and gateway sign, police department equipment purchases, a lengthy discussion on the future of tornado-damaged Fire Station 18, and ended with a major restructuring of the zoning department including the termination of the Planning and Zoning Director and hiring of a consulting firm. A new firefighter/paramedic was sworn in and a promotion was conducted. A resident from Ryan Homes raised concerns about increased zoning permit fees and slow turnaround times.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills from February 14-27, 2023 passed unanimously
Motion to purchase digital sign and gateway sign from One Stop Signs with installation for $49,950 from ARP account passed unanimously
Motion to approve purchase of Intoxilizer DMT from Intoximeter Incorporated not to exceed $13,000 passed unanimously
Motion to approve purchase of 2023 Chevrolet Blazer from Dave Keil Chevrolet not to exceed $36,155 passed unanimously
Motion to set special meeting zoning workshop for 3-16-23 at 6 PM passed unanimously
Motion to enter executive session at 7:06 PM passed unanimously
Motion to terminate employment of Planning and Zoning Director Harry Holbrook effective immediately passed unanimously
Motion to hire McBride Dale Clarion as planning and zoning consultant beginning March 1, 2023 at estimated $3,500/month passed unanimously
Motion to authorize Administrator Rose to assist zoning inspector in roles and powers passed unanimously
Motion to advertise for full-time zoning inspector position passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 8:08 PM passed unanimously
action_items: Chief Rhoads to verify no underground utilities and have digital sign location surveyed before installation
Chad Meadows to fill out ODOT permit for gateway sign at Deerfield and SR 28, expected 30-day turnaround
Chief Myers to order Intoxilizer DMT as soon as possible
Chief Myers to arrange meeting with insurance company, county building officials, architects, and engineers by March 6 regarding Station 18
Chief Myers to post a synopsis on fire department Facebook page about Station 18 status and link to presentation on website
Administrator Rose to meet with Paul Horst from Ryan Homes to review zoning permit fee numbers and discuss services
Mikayla to launch new Employee Assistance Program starting March 1
Mikayla to coordinate spring litter cleanup event on April 22, 2023 with Clermont County Soil and Water
Administrator Rose to begin working with McBride Dale Clarion consulting firm on zoning operations
Administrator Rose to advertise for full-time zoning inspector position
Trustees to consider recognition for American Idol contestant Mr. Hatfield from Goshen Township
Board to prepare for zoning workshop on March 16, 2023

$3,320,000 threshold needed to meet FEMA 50-50 rule for state funding
$460 per square foot estimate to build new station ($644,000 mentioned)
Up to $500+ per square foot possible for new station build
$700,000 estimated for parking lot, water lines, and sewer lines at Station 18
Over $1.6 million CDBG grant application submitted for Station 18
$3,500 estimated monthly cost for McBride Dale Clarion zoning consulting firm
$800,000 estimated out-of-pocket cost for repair/rebuild of damaged portion

attendees: Steve Pegram (former township employee, public speaker)
Paul Horst / Ryan Holmes (Ryan Homes representative, public speaker)
Adam Reed Daniel (new firefighter/paramedic sworn in)
Dan (Trustee Halderman, referenced by first name)
Rick Rhodes (resident, letter about Rollaway Drive potholes)
notes: A significant public safety incident occurred earlier in the day that was referenced multiple times but details were not disclosed. The meeting became notably tense during the Station 18 discussion with trustees expressing strong frustration about delays from insurance, county building officials, and engineers - nearly 9 months after the tornado with little progress. The termination of Planning and Zoning Director Harry Holbrook and complete restructuring of the zoning department was conducted after executive session, appearing to be directly related to the permit fee and service complaints raised earlier by the Ryan Homes representative. Director Holbrook was listed on tabled agenda items I through M but was reported as 'out ill.' The 2015 Ford Fusion from the police department will transfer to zoning department to replace a vehicle totaled in the tornado. Items I, J, K, L, M, and O were tabled.
